title: Default well-known database/cache/secret fields in Contro...
description:
# GitHub Issue #411: feat(c5c3-operator): default well-known database/cache/secret fields so a minimal ControlPlane CR shrinks

**Description:**

A managed-mode `ControlPlane` CR (`operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go`) today forces the user to spell out every database, cache, and admin-credential field even though, on the happy path, those values are fixed platform conventions that match the canonical sample byte-for-byte (`deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml`). This issue adds CRD/webhook defaults for those well-known fields so a minimal managed-mode CR can omit `spec.infrastructure` and the `spec.korc.adminCredential` body entirely and still validate and reconcile to `Ready`. The defaults live in the **c5c3 ControlPlane defaulting webhook** (`operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook.go`), not in the shared `commonv1` types — exactly the split the issue's design notes prescribe.

The mechanism is already proven in this repo. The defaulting webhook (`ControlPlaneWebhook.Default`, controlplane_webhook.go:72-100) already fills `spec.region`, `spec.korc.adminCredential.cloudCredentialsRef.secretName`, `applicationCredential.restricted`, and `rotation.mode` when they are zero-valued, and the integration helper `integrationManagedControlPlane` (integration_test.go:117) already relies on the webhook to fill those four. This issue extends `Default()` to populate the remaining seven well-known fields and constructs the full required subtree so a CR that omits `infrastructure`/`korc` passes the apiserver's `required` check.

Why "construct the subtree" works, and why the schema does **not** need to be relaxed: the mutating (defaulting) webhook is registered with `failurePolicy=fail` (controlplane_webhook.go:57). In the apiserver create pipeline the mutating webhook runs **before** structural-schema defaulting and the `required` check (which both run in the registry's `PrepareForCreate`), and `failurePolicy=fail` guarantees the webhook actually ran before validation. Because `ControlPlaneSpec.Infrastructure` and `ControlPlaneSpec.KORC` are **value types** (not pointers — controlplane_types.go:61,76), the decoded object always presents zero-valued `InfrastructureSpec{}`/`KORCSpec{}` structs for the webhook to fill, so `Default()` just writes the empty leaves and the post-webhook object satisfies the existing `required` markers (`required: [infrastructure, korc, openStackRelease, services]`, CRD lines 537-541). No CEL rules exist on the ControlPlane CRD (verified: zero `x-kubernetes-validations`), so the only post-webhook validation is the structural schema plus the validating webhook's XOR/required checks — all of which the populated object satisfies.

Concrete boundaries (what exists → what this issue adds):

1. **Defaulting entry point.** Already exists: `ControlPlaneWebhook.Default` (controlplane_webhook.go:72-100) with the constants `DefaultRegion` and `DefaultCloudCredentialsSecretName` (controlplane_webhook.go:29-35). This issue adds eight constants to the same const block — `DefaultDatabaseName="keystone"`, `DefaultDatabaseSecretName="keystone-db"`, `DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName="openstack-db"`, `DefaultCacheBackend="dogpile.cache.pymemcache"`, `DefaultCacheClusterRefName="openstack-memcached"`, `DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName="keystone-admin"`, `DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey="password"`, `DefaultCloudName="admin"` — and extends `Default()` to apply them.

2. **Database leaves (mode-neutral).** Already exists: `commonv1.DatabaseSpec` (`internal/common/types/types.go:21-41`) with required `Database` (db name) and `SecretRef`. This issue adds, in `Default()`: `if db.Database == "" → DefaultDatabaseName` and `if db.SecretRef.Name == "" → DefaultDatabaseSecretName`, where `db := &obj.Spec.Infrastructure.Database`. These apply in both managed and brownfield mode (they are not mode-sensitive). The issue table lists only `secretRef.name` for the DB secret, so `secretRef.key` is **not** defaulted here.

3. **Database managed clusterRef (mode-sensitive, brownfield-guarded).** Already exists: `reconcileInfrastructure` keys "managed mode" off `cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.ClusterRef != nil` (reconcile_infrastructure.go:89) and `ensureMariaDB` names the MariaDB child after `ClusterRef.Name` (reconcile_infrastructure.go:170). This issue adds the guarded default: only when `db.Host == ""` (the user did not choose brownfield), set `db.ClusterRef = &corev1.LocalObjectReference{Name: DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName}` if `db.ClusterRef == nil`, else fill `db.ClusterRef.Name` if empty. When `db.Host != ""` the webhook leaves `ClusterRef` nil so the validating webhook's XOR check (controlplane_webhook.go:152-159) still passes for brownfield. This is the issue's "Mode cannot be defaulted" constraint — the webhook never invents a mode over an explicit brownfield `host`.

4. **Cache leaves + managed clusterRef.** Already exists: `commonv1.CacheSpec` (`internal/common/types/types.go:85-101`, required `Backend`; `Replicas` already carries `+kubebuilder:default=3`). This issue adds, with `cache := &obj.Spec.Infrastructure.Cache`: `if cache.Backend == "" → DefaultCacheBackend`, and the brownfield-guarded clusterRef default keyed off `len(cache.Servers) == 0` → `DefaultCacheClusterRefName`. `cache.replicas` is unchanged (it already defaults to 3 via the shared marker, applied by schema defaulting after the webhook). This mirrors the keystone operator, whose own webhook already defaults `Cache.Backend` to `"dogpile.cache.pymemcache"` (keystone_webhook.go:124-125) — establishing that backend defaulting belongs in each operator's webhook, not a shared `commonv1` marker.

5. **Admin password Secret reference (shared type, webhook-only).** Already exists: `AdminCredentialSpec.PasswordSecretRef` is `commonv1.SecretRefSpec` (controlplane_types.go:193); the validating webhook requires `passwordSecretRef.name` (controlplane_webhook.go:174-179); `readAdminPassword` already falls back to data key `"password"` when `SecretRef.Key` is empty (reconcile_korc.go:798-805). This issue adds, with `korc := &obj.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential` (already aliased at controlplane_webhook.go:80): `if korc.PasswordSecretRef.Name == "" → DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName` and `if korc.PasswordSecretRef.Key == "" → DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey`. The `.name`/`.key` defaults are **not** added as `+kubebuilder:default` markers on `commonv1.SecretRefSpec` (that type is reused by the keystone operator at keystone_types.go:441 and a `keystone-*` default would leak) — webhook-only, exactly per the issue's design note. Defaulting `.key` makes the stored spec explicit and consistent with the reconciler's existing fallback; it also flows into the projected Keystone CR (`keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef = cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef`, reconcile_keystone.go:118).

6. **Cloud name (c5c3-local type, webhook + marker).** Already exists: `CloudCredentialsRef.CloudName` (controlplane_types.go:209-211) is a c5c3-local field, currently `required` with no default (CRD lines 345-348,357-358). Unlike the shared `SecretRefSpec` leaves, this is a c5c3-owned type, so it gets the same dual treatment as the sibling `SecretName`: add `+kubebuilder:default="admin"` plus `+optional` and `json:"cloudName,omitempty"`, and `if korc.CloudCredentialsRef.CloudName == "" → DefaultCloudName` in `Default()`. controller-gen then emits `default: admin` and drops `cloudName` from `cloudCredentialsRef.required`, exactly mirroring `SecretName` (controlplane_types.go:217-219; CRD lines 349-356).

7. **New import.** `controlplane_webhook.go` does not currently import `corev1`; constructing `&corev1.LocalObjectReference{...}` for the managed clusterRef defaults adds `corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"` to its import block.

8. **Generated artifacts.** Already exists: the CRD is generated into two synced copies — `operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml` and `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml` — via `make manifests` + `make sync-crds`, with `make verify-crd-sync` guarding drift (Makefile:238,259,284; `check-crd-drift` skill). This issue's only CRD delta is on `cloudName` (gains `default: admin`, leaves `required`); `make manifests sync-crds` must run and both copies be committed. `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` is unchanged (no struct shape change — `CloudName` stays `string`; only its json tag/markers change).

9. **No `commonv1` change.** Already exists: the keystone operator reuses `commonv1.DatabaseSpec`/`CacheSpec`/`SecretRefSpec` (keystone_types.go:72,77,441). This issue adds **zero** markers and **zero** field changes under `internal/common/types/`, so there is structurally no path to a keystone regression — the no-regression acceptance criterion is met by construction (`git diff` under `internal/common/types/` is empty).

The brownfield guard follows the project pattern "Avoid silent fallbacks on invalid post-admission state" (the webhook never invents a managed mode over an explicit `host`/`servers`); the webhook-plus-marker discipline for `cloudName` and the shared-type-stays-marker-free discipline follow "Apply defense-in-depth checks consistently to all similarly-marked fields" and the issue's own per-field decision; the generated-artifact step follows "Regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes"; the zero-value integration test follows "Test defaulting and validation together on zero-value objects".

**Motivation:**

A minimal managed-mode `ControlPlane` is ~30 lines of YAML today, but ~20 of those lines are platform constants that never change on the happy path — `openstack-db`, `keystone`, `keystone-db`, `dogpile.cache.pymemcache`, `openstack-memcached`, `keystone-admin`/`password`, `admin`. Every user copies them verbatim from the Quick Start (`docs/quick-start-controlplane.md:60-101`) and the kind sample (`deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml`). That hand-wiring is pure boilerplate, and boilerplate that everyone copies is boilerplate the operator should supply.

This surfaced while reworking the ControlPlane Quick Start to have the user author and `kubectl apply` the CR by hand (analogous to the per-service Keystone CR). With the per-service path, a minimal Keystone CR is genuinely small because the keystone operator already defaults the well-known fields in its own webhook (keystone_webhook.go defaults `cache.backend`, the rotation schedules, the resource requests, and more). The ControlPlane aggregate, however, still requires the user to repeat fields that the operator could fill, so the "minimal" ControlPlane Quick Start is three times longer than it needs to be and invites copy-paste drift.

Defaulting these fields in the c5c3 webhook shrinks the minimal managed-mode CR to roughly ten lines — `openStackRelease` plus `services.keystone` (with its gateway, which is genuinely site-specific and not defaulted). The defaults are field-level decisions, not blanket ones: the values are Keystone/OpenStack-specific conventions, so they belong in the c5c3 ControlPlane webhook, never in the generic `commonv1.SecretRefSpec`/`DatabaseSpec`/`CacheSpec` that the per-service Keystone operator shares (cf. #403). The operator still defaults only the *managed* `clusterRef` names and never over a brownfield `host`/`servers`, and the credential Secret *references* default to conventional names while the operator stays a pure consumer of OpenBao/ESO-seeded Secrets — it does not begin generating credentials.

**Affected Areas:**

- operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook.go — add eight `Default*` constants to the existing const block (controlplane_webhook.go:29-35); extend `ControlPlaneWebhook.Default` (controlplane_webhook.go:72-100) with the database, cache, password-Secret, and cloud-name defaults including the two brownfield-guarded managed `clusterRef` defaults; add the `corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"` import. `validate()` is unchanged (the `passwordSecretRef.name` required check at controlplane_webhook.go:174-179 stays as the defense-in-depth backstop the webhook now also satisfies).
- operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go — on `CloudCredentialsRef.CloudName` (controlplane_types.go:209-220) add `+kubebuilder:default="admin"` and `+optional`, change the tag to `json:"cloudName,omitempty"`, and add a DECISION comment mirroring the adjacent `SecretName` field.
- operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook_test.go — extend `TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults` (line 55), `TestDefault_IsIdempotent` (line 71), and `TestDefault_PreservesExplicitValues` (line 92) to cover the seven new fields; add a new `TestDefault_DoesNotInventModeForBrownfield` that sets `database.host`/`cache.servers` and asserts `ClusterRef` stays nil on both while the mode-neutral leaves (`database.database`, `database.secretRef.name`, `cache.backend`) are still defaulted.
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/integration_test.go — add a `integrationMinimalControlPlane(name, namespace)` helper (only `OpenStackRelease` + `Services.Keystone`, gateway optional) and a new `TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady` modeled on `TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady` (line 280): create the minimal CR through the envtest apiserver (proving the defaulting webhook satisfies `required`), then reuse `simulateMariaDBReadyWhenPresent`/`simulateMemcachedReadyWhenPresent` with keys `openstack-db`/`openstack-memcached` (the defaulted names) and drive `Ready=True`; assert the converged spec carries every default.
- operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml and operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml — regenerated via `make manifests sync-crds`: `cloudName` gains `default: admin` (CRD lines 345-348) and `cloudCredentialsRef.required` loses `cloudName` (CRD lines 357-358); both copies committed and `make verify-crd-sync` clean.
- docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md — update the `InfrastructureSpec` table (lines 199-202) and the `cloudCredentialsRef`/`passwordSecretRef` rows (lines 308,321-322) and the defaulting/validation-rules section (lines 555-575) to document every new default and its mechanism (webhook-only for the shared-type leaves and managed `clusterRef` names; webhook + `+kubebuilder:default` marker for `cloudName`).
- docs/quick-start-controlplane.md — replace the ~30-line example CR (lines 60-101) with the minimal form, optionally keeping the fully-explicit CR as a collapsible "equivalent expanded form".
- deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml — shrink to the minimal CR (keep `openStackRelease`, `services.keystone` with `replicas`/`gateway`, and the `publicEndpoint`/`KIND_HOST_PORT` comment), with a comment naming the operator-applied defaults (`openstack-db`, `keystone`, `keystone-db`, `openstack-memcached`, `keystone-admin`/`password`, `admin`) so the managed-mode provisioning stays documented and the `hack/deploy-infra.sh` `WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR=true` apply still works unchanged.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] A ControlPlane CR whose spec is only `openStackRelease: "2025.2"` plus `services.keystone` (with a `gateway` block) is accepted by the apiserver when the c5c3 defaulting webhook is active, and after admission its spec carries: `infrastructure.database.clusterRef.name=openstack-db`, `infrastructure.database.database=keystone`, `infrastructure.database.secretRef.name=keystone-db`, `infrastructure.cache.clusterRef.name=openstack-memcached`, `infrastructure.cache.backend=dogpile.cache.pymemcache`, `korc.adminCredential.cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName=admin`, `korc.adminCredential.passwordSecretRef.name=keystone-admin`, `korc.adminCredential.passwordSecretRef.key=password` — verified by the new `TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady`.
- [ ] The same minimal managed-mode CR reconciles to `Ready=True` on envtest (and on kind), driving the projected `openstack-db` MariaDB, `openstack-memcached` Memcached, projected Keystone, and K-ORC admin ApplicationCredential to Ready — the integration test asserts `InfrastructureReady`, `KeystoneReady`, `KORCReady`, `AdminCredentialReady`, `CatalogReady`, and aggregate `Ready` all True, matching `TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady`.
- [ ] `TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults` asserts all seven new defaults on a bare `&ControlPlane{}`, and `TestDefault_IsIdempotent` asserts a second `Default()` pass produces identical values for them.
- [ ] `TestDefault_DoesNotInventModeForBrownfield` asserts that with `database.host` set the webhook leaves `database.clusterRef == nil`, with `cache.servers` set it leaves `cache.clusterRef == nil`, and that `database.database`, `database.secretRef.name`, and `cache.backend` are still defaulted in both cases.
- [ ] `TestDefault_PreservesExplicitValues` asserts the webhook never overwrites an operator-supplied `database.clusterRef.name`, `database.database`, `database.secretRef.name`, `cache.clusterRef.name`, `cache.backend`, `passwordSecretRef.name`, `passwordSecretRef.key`, or `cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName`.
- [ ] Both CRD copies show `cloudName` with `default: admin` and absent from `cloudCredentialsRef.required`; `make verify-crd-sync` passes and `make manifests sync-crds` leaves no further diff.
- [ ] `docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md` documents every new default (the eight fields above) and names its mechanism (webhook-only vs webhook + marker); the `cloudName` row reads Required `No`, Default `admin`.
- [ ] `git diff` under `internal/common/types/` is empty, the keystone operator's `Default()`/tests are unchanged, and `make test` is green for both the `c5c3` and `keystone` modules.
- [ ] The Quick Start (`docs/quick-start-controlplane.md`) and kind sample (`deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml`) show the minimal CR, and `WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR=true make deploy-infra` still applies and reconciles it to Ready.

**Non-Goals:**

- Adding `+kubebuilder:default` markers to `commonv1.DatabaseSpec`, `commonv1.CacheSpec`, or `commonv1.SecretRefSpec` — those types are shared with the keystone operator (keystone_types.go:72,77,441) and a `keystone-*`/`openstack-*` default would leak; all shared-type defaults live in the c5c3 webhook only.
- Changing the keystone operator's own defaulting — its webhook already defaults `cache.backend` (keystone_webhook.go:124-125) and is untouched here.
- Inventing a database/cache *mode*: the webhook defaults only the managed `clusterRef` names and only when brownfield `host`/`servers` is unset, so a brownfield CR is never coerced into managed mode.
- Generating credentials: the Secret *references* default to conventional names, but the operator remains a consumer of OpenBao/ESO-seeded Secrets and does not mint or populate them.
- Defaulting `database.secretRef.key`: the issue table lists only `database.secretRef.name`; the DB secret's key convention is handled by the downstream projected Keystone CR and is out of scope.
- Changing `cache.replicas` defaulting: it already defaults to `3` via the shared `commonv1.CacheSpec.Replicas` marker (types.go:98) and is unchanged.
- Relaxing the database/cache XOR validation or the one-ControlPlane-per-namespace rule (controlplane_webhook.go:152-170,218-239).
- Promoting or further sharing `commonv1` types between the two operators — tracked separately in #403.

**References:**

- Issue #411 — feat(c5c3-operator): default well-known database/cache/secret fields (this issue).
- Related: #403 — promote/share `commonv1` types between both operators (the reason shared-type leaves stay marker-free).
- operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook.go — `ControlPlaneWebhook.Default` (72-100), constants (29-35), `failurePolicy=fail` marker (57), `validate()` XOR/required checks (135-205).
- operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go — `InfrastructureSpec` (83-93), `CloudCredentialsRef`/`SecretName` (209-220), `AdminCredentialSpec` (186-205), value-typed `Infrastructure`/`KORC` (61,76).
- internal/common/types/types.go — `DatabaseSpec` (21-41), `CacheSpec` (85-101), `SecretRefSpec` (104-107); do not add markers here.
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go — `Default()` defaults `Cache.Backend` (124-125), the per-operator-webhook precedent.
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go — reuse of `commonv1.DatabaseSpec`/`CacheSpec`/`SecretRefSpec` (72,77,441), the no-regression anchor.
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_infrastructure.go — managed-mode keying (89), `ensureMariaDB`/`ensureMemcached` clusterRef-name usage (170,229).
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go — `readAdminPassword` `"password"` fallback (798-805), `cloudName` usage (255,1127).
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go — `passwordSecretRef` projected into the Keystone bootstrap (118).
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/integration_test.go — `setupControlPlaneEnvTest` (70), `integrationManagedControlPlane` (117), `TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady` (280), simulator helpers (189-213).
- operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml — `cloudName`/`secretName` (345-356), `cloudCredentialsRef.required` (357-358), spec-level `required` (537-541).
- docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md (defaulting tables 199-202, 308, 321-322; defaulting/validation section 555-575), docs/quick-start-controlplane.md (60-101), deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml.
- Makefile — `manifests` (238), `sync-crds` (259), `verify-crd-sync` (284); `check-crd-drift` skill for CRD-sync verification.
- Review patterns: "Apply defense-in-depth checks consistently to all similarly-marked fields" (CC-0011), "Test defaulting and validation together on zero-value objects" (CC-0011), "Regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes" (CC-0013/CC-0038), "Avoid silent fallbacks on invalid post-admission state" (CC-0065), "Reuse exported constants instead of hardcoded duplicates" (CC-0096), "Fail closed when CRD validation can be bypassed" (CC-0039), and the technology patterns "Kubernetes API Conventions" and "Kubernetes Operator Reconciliation".
- Implementation note: tag the new constants, the `CloudName` DECISION comment, and the new tests with this issue's `CC-NNNN` feature marker (assigned per the repo's convention when the work is scheduled; GitHub issue #411) — do not invent the number ahead of assignment.

---

_Elaborated by [planwerk-review](https://github.com/planwerk/planwerk-review) with Claude Code_


**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/411

stories.title: Platform operator authors a minimal managed-mode ControlPlane (CC-0115)
stories.want: to apply a ControlPlane CR that only sets openStackRelease and services.keystone (with its site-specific gateway) and omit spec.infrastructure and spec.korc.adminCredential entirely
stories.so_that: I stop copy-pasting ~20 lines of fixed platform constants (openstack-db, keystone, keystone-db, dogpile.cache.pymemcache, openstack-memcached, keystone-admin/password, admin) that never change on the happy path
criteria: kubectl apply -f of a CR containing only spec.openStackRelease: "2025.2" and spec.services.keystone (with a gateway block) is ACCEPTED by the apiserver when the c5c3 defaulting webhook is active
After admission, kubectl get -o yaml shows infrastructure.database.clusterRef.name=openstack-db, infrastructure.database.database=keystone, infrastructure.database.secretRef.name=keystone-db
After admission the spec shows infrastructure.cache.clusterRef.name=openstack-memcached and infrastructure.cache.backend=dogpile.cache.pymemcache

After admission the spec shows korc.adminCredential.cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName=admin, korc.adminCredential.passwordSecretRef.name=keystone-admin, korc.adminCredential.passwordSecretRef.key=password
The defaulted CR reconciles to aggregate Ready=True (InfrastructureReady, KeystoneReady, KORCReady, AdminCredentialReady, CatalogReady all True)

stories.title: Brownfield operator keeps an explicit host/servers and is never coerced into managed mode (CC-0115)
stories.want: the defaulting webhook to leave database.clusterRef and cache.clusterRef nil whenever I set database.host or cache.servers
stories.so_that: the validating webhook's database/cache XOR check still passes and the operator never invents a managed mode over my explicit brownfield endpoint
criteria: With spec.infrastructure.database.host set and no clusterRef, after Default() database.clusterRef is still nil (no openstack-db invented)
With spec.infrastructure.cache.servers set and no clusterRef, after Default() cache.clusterRef is still nil (no openstack-memcached invented)
The mode-neutral leaves are STILL defaulted in brownfield mode: database.database=keystone, database.secretRef.name=keystone-db, cache.backend=dogpile.cache.pymemcache
A brownfield CR (host + servers + secretRefs set) still passes ValidateCreate without an XOR error

stories.title: Operator overrides any well-known default and the webhook preserves it (CC-0115)
stories.want: the defaulting webhook to never overwrite a value I supplied for any of the eight well-known fields
stories.so_that: I can point the control plane at a non-default database name, secret, cache backend, password Secret, key, or cloud entry when my environment requires it
criteria: An explicit database.database=mydb, database.secretRef.name=mydb-creds, database.clusterRef.name=my-db are all preserved (not overwritten to the defaults)
An explicit cache.backend=dogpile.cache.memcached and cache.clusterRef.name=my-cache are preserved
An explicit passwordSecretRef.name=my-admin, passwordSecretRef.key=adminpw, and cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName=operator are preserved
A second Default() pass over an already-defaulted object produces byte-identical values for all eight fields (idempotent)

stories.title: Operator-engineer extends the defaulting webhook with shared exported constants (CC-0115)
stories.want: the eight new default values declared as exported Default* constants in the same const block as DefaultRegion/DefaultCloudCredentialsSecretName and reused by Default(), tests, and (where relevant) the integration test
stories.so_that: the default strings have a single source of truth and cannot drift between the webhook, the tests, and the projected children
criteria: controlplane_webhook.go declares DefaultDatabaseName, DefaultDatabaseSecretName, DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName, DefaultCacheBackend, DefaultCacheClusterRefName, DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName, DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey, DefaultCloudName in the existing const block
Default() references the constants (no inline literal duplicates) and constructs &corev1.LocalObjectReference{Name: DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName} for the managed clusterRef default
controlplane_webhook.go imports corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
go vet and the c5c3 unit suite pass with the constants in use

stories.title: Release/CI engineer keeps both generated CRD copies in sync (CC-0115)
stories.want: make manifests sync-crds regenerated and both CRD copies (config/crd/bases and helm/c5c3-operator/crds) committed, with cloudName gaining default: admin and dropping from cloudCredentialsRef.required
stories.so_that: make verify-crd-sync stays clean and the cluster never rejects a CR because the installed CRD schema lags the controller
criteria: operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml shows cloudName with `default: admin` and cloudCredentialsRef.required no longer lists cloudName
operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml carries the identical schema (only the cross-reference comment header differs)
make verify-crd-sync exits 0
A second make manifests sync-crds run leaves no further git diff (generation is stable)
zz_generated.deepcopy.go is unchanged (CloudName stays a string; only its json tag/markers change)

stories.title: Quick-start reader and kind user get a minimal CR that reconciles (CC-0115)
stories.want: the ControlPlane Quick Start and the kind sample to show the minimal CR (openStackRelease + services.keystone with gateway), with a comment naming the operator-applied defaults
stories.so_that: the minimal Quick Start is ~3x shorter, free of copy-paste drift, and the WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR=true deploy path still applies and reconciles it
criteria: docs/quick-start-controlplane.md Step 3 example CR omits region, infrastructure, and the korc.adminCredential body (optionally keeping the fully-explicit form as a collapsible "equivalent expanded form")

deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml keeps openStackRelease and services.keystone (replicas + gateway) and the publicEndpoint/KIND_HOST_PORT comment, with a comment listing the operator-applied defaults
The hack/deploy-infra.sh yq publicEndpoint injection (selects kind==ControlPlane and sets .spec.services.keystone.publicEndpoint) still works against the minimal CR
WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR=true make deploy-infra applies the CR and it reconciles to Ready (live-cluster verification; sandbox-blocked — see risks)

stories.title: Keystone-operator maintainer sees zero regression from the shared commonv1 types (CC-0115)
stories.want: all eight defaults to live in the c5c3 webhook only, with no +kubebuilder:default markers and no field changes under internal/common/types/
stories.so_that: the keystone operator that reuses commonv1.DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/SecretRefSpec never inherits a leaked keystone-*/openstack-* default and its own Default()/tests stay untouched
criteria: git diff under internal/common/types/ is empty after the change
operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go Default() and keystone webhook tests are unchanged
make test is green for BOTH the c5c3 and keystone modules
No +kubebuilder:default marker is added to commonv1.DatabaseSpec, CacheSpec, or SecretRefSpec

requirements.description: The c5c3 ControlPlane defaulting webhook SHALL default the mode-neutral database leaves database.database to "keystone" and database.secretRef.name to "keystone-db" when they are empty, in BOTH managed and brownfield mode. It SHALL NOT default database.secretRef.key.
requirements.rationale: These two leaves are fixed platform conventions matching the canonical sample byte-for-byte and are not mode-sensitive; secretRef.key is explicitly out of scope per the issue table.

scenarios.name: Zero-valued database leaves defaulted
scenarios.when: Default() runs on a ControlPlane with empty database.database and database.secretRef.name
scenarios.then: database.database becomes "keystone" and database.secretRef.name becomes "keystone-db"
and_then: database.secretRef.key is left empty (not defaulted)

scenarios.name: Defaulted in brownfield mode too
scenarios.when: Default() runs on a CR with database.host set (brownfield) and empty leaves
scenarios.then: database.database and database.secretRef.name are still defaulted
and_then: the brownfield host is preserved

scenarios.name: Explicit leaves preserved
scenarios.when: Default() runs on a CR with database.database="mydb" and database.secretRef.name="mydb-creds"
scenarios.then: both values are left untouched
and_then: a second Default() pass produces the same values

requirements.description: The webhook SHALL default the managed database clusterRef name to "openstack-db" ONLY when database.host is empty (managed mode). When database.host is set (brownfield) it SHALL leave database.clusterRef nil so the validating webhook's database XOR check still passes.
requirements.rationale: Mode cannot be defaulted: the webhook must never invent a managed clusterRef over an explicit brownfield host (project pattern: avoid silent fallbacks on invalid post-admission state).

scenarios.name: Managed clusterRef created when host empty
scenarios.when: Default() runs with database.host empty and database.clusterRef nil
scenarios.then: database.clusterRef is set to &corev1.LocalObjectReference{Name: "openstack-db"}
and_then: the XOR check (exactly one of clusterRef/host) passes

scenarios.name: Existing clusterRef name filled, not replaced
scenarios.when: Default() runs with database.host empty and database.clusterRef non-nil but Name empty
scenarios.then: only database.clusterRef.Name is set to "openstack-db"
and_then: an existing non-empty Name is preserved

scenarios.name: Brownfield host leaves clusterRef nil
scenarios.when: Default() runs with database.host set
scenarios.then: database.clusterRef stays nil
and_then: the validating webhook does not report a database XOR violation

requirements.description: The webhook SHALL default cache.backend to "dogpile.cache.pymemcache" when empty (both modes), and SHALL default the managed cache clusterRef name to "openstack-memcached" ONLY when cache.servers is empty (managed mode), leaving cache.clusterRef nil when cache.servers is set. cache.replicas is unchanged (already defaults to 3 via the shared commonv1 marker).
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the keystone operator's own webhook (keystone_webhook.go:124-125) which defaults cache.backend; the clusterRef brownfield guard mirrors the database guard.

scenarios.name: Backend and managed clusterRef defaulted
scenarios.when: Default() runs with empty cache.backend and empty cache.servers and nil cache.clusterRef
scenarios.then: cache.backend becomes "dogpile.cache.pymemcache" and cache.clusterRef becomes &corev1.LocalObjectReference{Name: "openstack-memcached"}
and_then: cache.replicas is left to the schema default (3), not set by the webhook

scenarios.name: Brownfield servers leaves clusterRef nil
scenarios.when: Default() runs with cache.servers non-empty
scenarios.then: cache.clusterRef stays nil
and_then: cache.backend is still defaulted
the cache XOR check passes

scenarios.name: Explicit backend/clusterRef preserved
scenarios.when: Default() runs with cache.backend="dogpile.cache.memcached" and cache.clusterRef.Name="my-cache"
scenarios.then: both are preserved
and_then: idempotent on a second pass

requirements.description: The webhook SHALL default korc.adminCredential.passwordSecretRef.name to "keystone-admin" and passwordSecretRef.key to "password" when empty. These defaults SHALL be webhook-only — NO +kubebuilder:default marker is added to commonv1.SecretRefSpec.
requirements.rationale: SecretRefSpec is reused by the keystone operator (keystone_types.go:441); a keystone-* default would leak. Defaulting .key makes the stored spec explicit and consistent with the reconciler's existing readAdminPassword "password" fallback (reconcile_korc.go:797-805); the value flows into the projected Keystone bootstrap (reconcile_keystone.go:118).

scenarios.name: Password secret ref defaulted
scenarios.when: Default() runs with empty passwordSecretRef.name and key
scenarios.then: name becomes "keystone-admin" and key becomes "password"
and_then: the validating webhook's required passwordSecretRef.name check is satisfied (the webhook now also satisfies it, the check stays as defense-in-depth)

scenarios.name: Explicit password secret ref preserved
scenarios.when: Default() runs with passwordSecretRef.name="my-admin" and key="adminpw"
scenarios.then: both are preserved
and_then: idempotent on a second pass

scenarios.name: No shared-type marker
scenarios.when: the change is inspected
scenarios.then: internal/common/types/types.go has no +kubebuilder:default on SecretRefSpec
and_then: git diff under internal/common/types/ is empty

requirements.description: The c5c3-local CloudCredentialsRef.CloudName SHALL default to "admin" via BOTH a +kubebuilder:default="admin" marker (with +optional and json:"cloudName,omitempty") AND the defaulting webhook, mirroring the sibling SecretName field. controller-gen SHALL then emit default: admin and drop cloudName from cloudCredentialsRef.required.
requirements.rationale: CloudName is a c5c3-owned type (not shared commonv1), so it gets the same dual webhook+marker treatment as SecretName; the value default "admin" matches the reconciler's korcAdminUsername fallback (reconcile_korc.go:256-257,683) and flows into the User/Service/Endpoint refs (reconcile_korc.go:371,375,1127).

scenarios.name: CloudName defaulted by webhook
scenarios.when: Default() runs with empty cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName
scenarios.then: cloudName becomes "admin"
and_then: a CR that omitted the whole korc block has cloudName=admin after admission

scenarios.name: CRD marker emitted
scenarios.when: make manifests runs
scenarios.then: the CRD shows cloudName with default: admin and absent from cloudCredentialsRef.required
and_then: both the config/crd/bases and helm copies carry it

scenarios.name: Explicit cloudName preserved
scenarios.when: Default() runs with cloudName="operator"
scenarios.then: the value is preserved
and_then: idempotent on a second pass

requirements.description: A managed-mode ControlPlane whose spec is only openStackRelease plus services.keystone (with a gateway block) SHALL be ACCEPTED by the envtest apiserver with the defaulting webhook active, and after admission its spec SHALL carry all eight defaulted values listed in the acceptance criteria.
requirements.rationale: The mutating webhook runs before structural-schema defaulting and the required check; because Infrastructure and KORC are value types the decoded object presents zero-valued structs the webhook fills, so the populated object satisfies the existing spec-level required markers (infrastructure, korc, openStackRelease, services) and all nested required leaves. There are zero CEL rules on the CRD.

scenarios.name: Minimal CR accepted
scenarios.when: TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady creates the minimal CR via the envtest apiserver
scenarios.then: c.Create succeeds (required check passes after the webhook constructs the subtree)
and_then: the converged spec carries every default

scenarios.name: All eight defaults present post-admission
scenarios.when: the created CR is re-fetched
scenarios.then: database.clusterRef.name=openstack-db, database.database=keystone, database.secretRef.name=keystone-db, cache.clusterRef.name=openstack-memcached, cache.backend=dogpile.cache.pymemcache, cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName=admin, passwordSecretRef.name=keystone-admin, passwordSecretRef.key=password
and_then: region is also RegionOne via the pre-existing default

scenarios.name: Webhook genuinely required
scenarios.when: the minimal CR is created
scenarios.then: acceptance proves the webhook ran (a bare struct without the webhook would fail the nested required checks for the empty database/cache/passwordSecretRef leaves)
and_then: the test documents the mechanism in a comment with the CC-0115 marker

requirements.description: The minimal managed-mode ControlPlane SHALL reconcile to aggregate Ready=True on envtest, driving the projected openstack-db MariaDB, openstack-memcached Memcached, projected Keystone, and K-ORC admin ApplicationCredential to Ready, asserting InfrastructureReady, KeystoneReady, KORCReady, AdminCredentialReady, CatalogReady, and aggregate Ready all True.
requirements.rationale: Proves the defaulted clusterRef names (openstack-db/openstack-memcached) are exactly what the simulator helpers key off, so the defaulting and the reconcile chain agree end-to-end (matching TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady).

scenarios.name: Chain reaches Ready
scenarios.when: the test simulates MariaDB/Memcached/Keystone/AC/PushSecret readiness in dependency order using keys openstack-db/openstack-memcached
scenarios.then: the five sub-conditions and the aggregate Ready all reach True
and_then: Ready reason is AllReady

scenarios.name: Projected children carry defaults
scenarios.when: the projected Keystone CR is fetched
scenarios.then: its Database.ClusterRef.Name=openstack-db and Cache.ClusterRef.Name=openstack-memcached (projected verbatim from the defaulted infra)
and_then: the AC mints with the operator-owned password cloud and restricted defaults to true

scenarios.name: Admin password Secret resolves with default key
scenarios.when: the keystone-admin Secret with data key "password" is present
scenarios.then: readAdminPassword resolves it via the defaulted passwordSecretRef.key=password
and_then: KORCReady advances

requirements.description: The defaulting webhook SHALL be idempotent for all eight new fields and SHALL preserve every operator-supplied value for them. The unit tests SHALL assert all eight defaults on a bare &ControlPlane{}, idempotency on a second pass, brownfield non-invention, and explicit-value preservation.
requirements.rationale: Tests defaulting and validation together on zero-value objects (project pattern CC-0011); the *bool/pointer/empty-string discrimination must not regress.

scenarios.name: Bare object gets all defaults
scenarios.when: TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults runs Default() on &ControlPlane{}
scenarios.then: all seven new defaults (plus the pre-existing four) are asserted present
and_then: database.secretRef.key remains empty (not defaulted)

scenarios.name: Second pass identical
scenarios.when: TestDefault_IsIdempotent runs Default() twice
scenarios.then: the seven new fields are byte-identical between passes

scenarios.name: Explicit values preserved
scenarios.when: TestDefault_PreservesExplicitValues sets all eight fields explicitly
scenarios.then: none are overwritten

scenarios.name: Brownfield mode not invented
scenarios.when: TestDefault_DoesNotInventModeForBrownfield sets database.host and cache.servers
scenarios.then: both clusterRefs stay nil while database.database, database.secretRef.name and cache.backend are still defaulted

requirements.description: The change SHALL make ZERO modifications under internal/common/types/ and SHALL leave the keystone operator's Default()/tests unchanged. make test SHALL be green for both the c5c3 and keystone modules.
requirements.rationale: The no-regression criterion is met by construction: all defaults live in the c5c3 webhook only, so there is structurally no path to a keystone regression.

scenarios.name: Shared types untouched
scenarios.when: git diff internal/common/types/ is run after the change
scenarios.then: the diff is empty

scenarios.name: Both modules green
scenarios.when: make test runs for c5c3 and keystone
scenarios.then: both pass
and_then: keystone Default() behavior is unchanged

scenarios.name: Workspace deps unchanged
scenarios.when: the import of corev1 is added to controlplane_webhook.go
scenarios.then: no go.mod change is required (corev1 already a transitive/declared dep of the api package)
and_then: go mod tidy under operators/c5c3 introduces no new require beyond existing

requirements.description: Both generated CRD copies SHALL be regenerated via make manifests sync-crds and committed; cloudName SHALL show default: admin and be absent from cloudCredentialsRef.required in both copies; make verify-crd-sync SHALL pass; a re-run SHALL leave no further diff.
requirements.rationale: Regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes (project pattern CC-0013/CC-0038); check-crd-drift discipline.

scenarios.name: cloudName delta present in both copies
scenarios.when: make manifests sync-crds runs
scenarios.then: config/crd/bases and helm/c5c3-operator/crds both show cloudName default: admin and the trimmed required list
and_then: the only schema delta vs HEAD is on cloudName

scenarios.name: Sync clean
scenarios.when: make verify-crd-sync runs
scenarios.then: it exits 0

scenarios.name: Generation stable
scenarios.when: make manifests sync-crds is run twice
scenarios.then: the second run leaves no git diff
and_then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go is unchanged

requirements.description: docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md SHALL document every new default and name its mechanism: webhook-only for the shared-type leaves (database.database, database.secretRef.name, cache.backend, passwordSecretRef.name, passwordSecretRef.key) and the managed clusterRef names (database.clusterRef.name, cache.clusterRef.name); webhook + +kubebuilder:default marker for cloudName. The cloudName row SHALL read Required No, Default admin.
requirements.rationale: Documentation must track the new defaulting behavior and the deliberate webhook-only vs webhook+marker split (Diataxis Reference quadrant for the CRD).

scenarios.name: Defaulting table extended
scenarios.when: the Defaulting Webhook table (controlplane-crd.md:650-655) is updated
scenarios.then: it lists all eight new field/condition/default rows with their mechanism
and_then: the brownfield guard for the two clusterRef rows is noted

scenarios.name: cloudName row updated
scenarios.when: the CloudCredentialsRef table (controlplane-crd.md:319-322) is updated
scenarios.then: cloudName reads Required No, Default admin
and_then: the Validating-webhook rules / required notes reflect cloudName no longer being required

scenarios.name: InfrastructureSpec narrative updated
scenarios.when: the InfrastructureSpec section (controlplane-crd.md:199-209) is updated
scenarios.then: it documents the webhook-supplied database/cache defaults and that a minimal CR may omit infrastructure

requirements.description: docs/quick-start-controlplane.md and deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml SHALL show the minimal CR (openStackRelease + services.keystone with replicas/gateway), with a comment naming the operator-applied defaults, and the WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR=true deploy path SHALL still apply and reconcile it.
requirements.rationale: The minimal CR is the user-facing motivation; the kind sample must stay deploy-able and the publicEndpoint yq injection (selecting .spec.services.keystone) must keep working.

scenarios.name: Quick Start minimal CR
scenarios.when: the Step 3 example (quick-start-controlplane.md:60-101) is replaced
scenarios.then: it omits region, infrastructure and the korc.adminCredential body (optionally keeping the expanded form collapsible)
and_then: the prose still names the seeded Secrets the operator consumes

scenarios.name: kind sample minimal CR
scenarios.when: deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml is shrunk
scenarios.then: it keeps openStackRelease and services.keystone (replicas + gateway) and the publicEndpoint/KIND_HOST_PORT comment plus a defaults comment
and_then: the yq selector .kind==ControlPlane sets .spec.services.keystone.publicEndpoint successfully

scenarios.name: deploy path unchanged
scenarios.when: WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR=true make deploy-infra runs on a live cluster
scenarios.then: the minimal CR is applied and reconciles to Ready
and_then: this is a live-cluster check, sandbox-blocked — see risks/deferred

requirements.description: The eight default values SHALL be declared as exported Default* constants in the existing const block (controlplane_webhook.go:29-35) and reused by Default() (and the gosec G101 nolint applied to the two Secret-name constants as the existing block does), with no hardcoded literal duplicates at call sites.
requirements.rationale: Reuse exported constants instead of hardcoded duplicates (project pattern CC-0096); keeps the webhook the single source of truth for the default strings.

scenarios.name: Constants declared
scenarios.when: the const block is extended
scenarios.then: DefaultDatabaseName, DefaultDatabaseSecretName, DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName, DefaultCacheBackend, DefaultCacheClusterRefName, DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName, DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey, DefaultCloudName exist
and_then: the two Secret-name constants carry the //nolint:gosec G101 comment like DefaultCloudCredentialsSecretName

scenarios.name: No literal duplicates
scenarios.when: Default() is inspected
scenarios.then: every default value is referenced via its constant
and_then: the tests may also reference the constants for their assertions

scenarios.name: Lint clean
scenarios.when: golangci-lint runs
scenarios.then: no G101 or unused-constant findings

tasks.title: Add eight exported Default* constants (CC-0115) to the const block in operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook.go (DefaultDatabaseName="keystone", DefaultDatabaseSecretName="keystone-db", DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName="openstack-db", DefaultCacheBackend="dogpile.cache.pymemcache", DefaultCacheClusterRefName="openstack-memcached", DefaultAdminPasswordSecretName="keystone-admin", DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey="password", DefaultCloudName="admin"); add the //nolint:gosec G101 comment to the two Secret-name constants exactly as DefaultCloudCredentialsSecretName does. Verify: go build ./... in operators/c5c3.

Edit CloudCredentialsRef.CloudName in operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go (lines 209-211): add +kubebuilder:default="admin" and +optional markers, change the tag to json:"cloudName,omitempty", and add a DECISION (CC-0115) comment mirroring the adjacent SecretName field. Verify: go build; the struct shape is unchanged (CloudName stays string).

Extend ControlPlaneWebhook.Default (controlplane_webhook.go:72-100) with the DATABASE defaults: db := &obj.Spec.Infrastructure.Database; default db.Database and db.SecretRef.Name when empty; and the brownfield-guarded managed clusterRef — only when db.Host=="", set db.ClusterRef=&corev1.LocalObjectReference{Name: DefaultDatabaseClusterRefName} if nil else fill empty .Name. Add the corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1" import. Verify: go build (CC-0115).

Extend Default() with the CACHE defaults: cache := &obj.Spec.Infrastructure.Cache; default cache.Backend when empty; brownfield-guarded managed clusterRef — only when len(cache.Servers)==0, set/fill cache.ClusterRef.Name=DefaultCacheClusterRefName. Leave cache.Replicas untouched. Verify: go build (CC-0115).

Extend Default() with the ADMIN-CREDENTIAL defaults on the existing korc:=&obj.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential alias: default korc.PasswordSecretRef.Name and korc.PasswordSecretRef.Key when empty; default korc.CloudCredentialsRef.CloudName when empty. Verify: go build; go vet (CC-0115).

Extend TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults (controlplane_webhook_test.go:55) and TestDefault_IsIdempotent (:71) to assert the seven new defaults on a bare &ControlPlane{} and their idempotency, referencing the Default* constants; confirm database.secretRef.key stays empty. Verify: go test ./api/... passes (CC-0115).

Extend TestDefault_PreservesExplicitValues (:92) to set and assert preservation of database.clusterRef.name, database.database, database.secretRef.name, cache.clusterRef.name, cache.backend, passwordSecretRef.name, passwordSecretRef.key, cloudCredentialsRef.cloudName. Verify: go test ./api/... passes (CC-0115).

Add TestDefault_DoesNotInventModeForBrownfield (CC-0115) to controlplane_webhook_test.go: case A sets database.host and asserts database.clusterRef stays nil; case B sets cache.servers and asserts cache.clusterRef stays nil; both assert database.database, database.secretRef.name and cache.backend are still defaulted. Verify: go test ./api/... passes.

Run make manifests sync-crds; commit the regenerated operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml and operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml. Confirm cloudName gains default: admin (CRD ~line 345-348) and cloudCredentialsRef.required drops cloudName (~357-358); confirm zz_generated.deepcopy.go is unchanged. Verify: make verify-crd-sync exits 0 and a re-run leaves no diff (CC-0115, REQ-010).

Add integrationMinimalControlPlane(name, namespace) helper to operators/c5c3/internal/controller/integration_test.go: a ControlPlane with ONLY Spec.OpenStackRelease="2025.2" and Spec.Services.Keystone (Replicas ptr; gateway optional) — infrastructure and korc omitted (zero structs) so the defaulting webhook must construct them. Verify: go build -tags integration (CC-0115).

Add TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady (CC-0115) modeled on TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady (:280): create the minimal CR through the envtest apiserver (asserting Create succeeds — required satisfied by the webhook), re-fetch and assert all eight defaults on the converged spec, then reuse simulateMariaDBReadyWhenPresent/simulateMemcachedReadyWhenPresent (keys openstack-db/openstack-memcached) + Keystone/AC/PushSecret simulators to drive every sub-condition and aggregate Ready=True; assert the projected Keystone CR's database/cache clusterRefs are openstack-db/openstack-memcached. Verify: go test -tags integration ./internal/controller/... passes under envtest.

Update docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md: extend the Defaulting Webhook table (:650-655) with the eight new field/condition/default rows naming each mechanism (webhook-only for the shared-type leaves + the two managed clusterRef names with the brownfield guard noted; webhook + +kubebuilder:default for cloudName); update the InfrastructureSpec narrative (:199-209), the AdminCredentialSpec passwordSecretRef row (:308) and the CloudCredentialsRef cloudName row (:319-322) to Required No / Default admin; adjust the Validating-webhook rules / required notes so cloudName is no longer listed required. Tag edits with CC-0115. Verify: links/anchors intact, table renders.

Update docs/quick-start-controlplane.md Step 3 (:60-101): replace the ~30-line example CR with the minimal form (openStackRelease + services.keystone with gateway/publicEndpoint), optionally keeping the fully-explicit CR as a collapsible "equivalent expanded form"; keep the prose noting the operator consumes (does not invent) the seeded Secrets. Tag with CC-0115. Verify: YAML is valid and matches the kind sample's minimal shape.

Shrink deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml to the minimal CR: keep openStackRelease and services.keystone (replicas: 1 + gateway) and the publicEndpoint/KIND_HOST_PORT comment; remove region, infrastructure and the korc block; add a comment naming the operator-applied defaults (openstack-db, keystone, keystone-db, dogpile.cache.pymemcache, openstack-memcached, keystone-admin/password, admin). Tag with CC-0115. Verify: yq selector `.kind=="ControlPlane"` can still set .spec.services.keystone.publicEndpoint (the deploy-infra.sh injection path).

Run the full module suites and the no-regression guards: `make test` (or go test) for the c5c3 module (unit + integration) and the keystone module; confirm `git diff -- internal/common/types/` is empty and the keystone webhook/tests are untouched. Verify: both modules green; shared-types diff empty (CC-0115, REQ-009).

test_specifications.story: Keystone-operator maintainer sees zero regression from the shared commonv1 types (CC-0115)

review_criteria: BLOCKER: A minimal managed-mode CR (openStackRelease + services.keystone) is accepted by the envtest apiserver with the defaulting webhook active and post-admission carries all eight defaults (REQ-006) — proven by TestIntegration_MinimalManagedToReady.

BLOCKER: git diff under internal/common/types/ is empty and no +kubebuilder:default marker was added to commonv1.DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/SecretRefSpec; the keystone module's Default()/tests are unchanged and make test is green for both modules (REQ-009).

BLOCKER: The brownfield guard holds — with database.host set database.clusterRef stays nil, with cache.servers set cache.clusterRef stays nil, and the validating webhook's database/cache XOR checks still pass (REQ-002, REQ-003).

implementation_notes:
FEATURE ID: CC-0115 (GitHub #411). Backend-only Go operator change (kubebuilder + controller-runtime); there is NO frontend layer, so the fullstack/UI checklist is N/A.

MECHANISM (why constructing the subtree works and the schema needs no relaxing): The mutating (defaulting) webhook is registered failurePolicy=fail (controlplane_webhook.go:57) and runs BEFORE structural-schema defaulting and the required check (both in the registry's PrepareForCreate). ControlPlaneSpec.Infrastructure and ControlPlaneSpec.KORC are VALUE types (controlplane_types.go:61,76), so the decoded object always presents zero-valued InfrastructureSpec{}/KORCSpec{} structs for Default() to fill. Default() writes the empty leaves so the post-webhook object satisfies the existing spec-level required markers [infrastructure, korc, openStackRelease, services] (CRD:537-541) AND the nested required leaves (database.database, database.secretRef.name, cache.backend, passwordSecretRef.name). There are ZERO x-kubernetes-validations (CEL) rules on the CRD (verified), so the only post-webhook validation is the structural schema + the validating webhook's XOR/required checks — all satisfied by the populated object.

IMPORTANT NUANCE — why cloudName needs the webhook AND the marker: CRD schema defaulting (the `default: admin` marker) only fires when the parent object exists. When the whole `korc` block is omitted, no `default:` exists on korc/adminCredential/cloudCredentialsRef objects, so the marker alone would never reach cloudName. The mutating webhook (operating on the always-present Go value struct) is the load-bearing mechanism for the fully-omitted case; the marker is consistency/defense-in-depth for the case where cloudCredentialsRef is present but cloudName omitted. This mirrors the existing SecretName treatment exactly.

BROWNFIELD GUARD (the issue's 'Mode cannot be defaulted' constraint): only set the managed clusterRef when the brownfield discriminator is unset — db.Host=="" for database, len(cache.Servers)==0 for cache. Pattern: if guard-clear { if ref==nil { ref=&corev1.LocalObjectReference{Name: DEFAULT} } else if ref.Name=="" { ref.Name=DEFAULT } }. This keeps the validating webhook's XOR ((clusterRef!=nil)==(host!="")) passing in brownfield mode.

WEBHOOK-ONLY vs WEBHOOK+MARKER split: shared commonv1 leaves (DatabaseSpec.Database/SecretRef.Name, CacheSpec.Backend, SecretRefSpec.Name/Key) get webhook-only defaults — NEVER a +kubebuilder:default marker — because keystone reuses those types (keystone_types.go:72,77,441) and a keystone-*/openstack-* default would leak (cf. #403). The c5c3-local CloudName gets the dual webhook+marker treatment like its sibling SecretName.

VALUE-CONSISTENCY anchors: DefaultCloudName="admin" matches reconcile_korc.go korcAdminUsername ("admin", :683) and its empty-cloudName fallback (:256-257); DefaultAdminPasswordSecretKey="password" matches readAdminPassword's fallback (:797-805); all eight values match deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml byte-for-byte and the e2e fixture (tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/00-controlplane-cr.yaml).

DATA FLOW (managed minimal CR):
  kubectl apply (minimal YAML: openStackRelease + services.keystone)
    -> apiserver decode -> MUTATING webhook Default(): fills region + infra leaves + db/cache clusterRefs + passwordSecretRef.name/.key + cloudName + restricted + rotation.mode
    -> structural-schema defaulting (cache.replicas=3, cloudName/secretName markers) + required check (PASSES)
    -> VALIDATING webhook validate(): XOR(db)=pass, XOR(cache)=pass, passwordSecretRef.name=set, release pattern=ok
    -> persisted
    -> reconcileInfrastructure: dbManaged=clusterRef!=nil -> ensureMariaDB(openstack-db) + ensureMemcached(openstack-memcached)
    -> reconcileKeystone: projects Database/Cache verbatim + bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef=passwordSecretRef
    -> reconcileKORC: reads keystone-admin/password, mints admin AC with cloudName=admin
    -> AdminCredential push + Catalog (Service/Endpoint) -> aggregate Ready=True.

PITFALLS: (1) Do NOT add markers to commonv1 — verify git diff internal/common/types is empty before finishing. (2) The two Secret-name constants need the //nolint:gosec G101 comment (gosec flags Secret-name string literals). (3) Keep make manifests sync-crds output stable — run twice and confirm no diff; verify zz_generated.deepcopy.go is untouched (json-tag/marker change only). (4) The integration test must construct the helper as a Go struct with zero Infrastructure/KORC; controller-runtime serializes value structs as present-but-empty objects, which decode back to zero structs the webhook fills — same outcome as omitted YAML keys. (5) e2e Chainsaw fixtures set cloudName explicitly; the CRD change only relaxes (optional, dropped from required), so they remain valid and are intentionally left unchanged.

DEFERRED ITEMS: see deferred_items.
